Client 2: And I’m Margare. I just want us to feel a little more… organized, I guess, and not surprised by anything.
Advisor: That makes sense. We’ll keep it practical. Let’s start with the big picture income—roughly what’s the household gross income per year right now?
Client 1: Household gross is 87500 a year.
Advisor: Got it—thank you. And just so I’m not mixing you up, William you’re Client 1 and Margare you’re Client 2, right?
Client 2: Yes—William is Client 1, and I’m Client 2.

Client 2: Yes—William is Client 1, and I’m Client 2.
Advisor: Perfect. Now, expenses—on average, what do you two spend per month to run the household? Mortgage or rent, utilities, food, all of it.
Client 2: We’re at 4250 a month, give or take.
Advisor: Okay. And debt payments—credit cards, car loans, student loans—what’s the total you’re paying monthly toward debt?
Client 1: Zero. Monthly debt payments total is 0.

Client 2: And I’m Margare. I just want us to feel a little more… organized, I guess, and not surprised by anything.

Client 1: Zero. Monthly debt payments total is 0.
Advisor: Nice. One sec—just noting that down. On the asset side, what do you consider your investable assets total—things like brokerage, retirement accounts, cash you’d invest?
Client 1: Investable assets total is 431000.
Advisor: Great. We’ll unpack what’s inside that in a minute. Before we do, do either of you have any loans outstanding at all right now—mortgage, HELOC, anything like that?
Client 2: No, none. No loans outstanding.

Client 1: Yes, private markets on a retirement platform—38600.
Advisor: Great. Margare, do you have any assets listed separately in your name—like the home?
Client 2: The primary residence is in my name on the paperwork we have here—value is 326150.
Advisor: Understood. And just to be clear, that’s the home value, not an investment account—right?
Client 2: Right, it’s the house value—326150.

Advisor: Hi William, hi Margare—nice to meet you both. I’m glad you made the time. Before we get into numbers, what prompted you to set this up now?
Client 1: Thanks. I’m William. Honestly, it’s the self-employed income swings—some months feel tight even if the year looks fine.
Client 2: And I’m Margare. I just want us to feel a little more… organized, I guess, and not surprised by anything.
Advisor: That makes sense. We’ll keep it practical. Let’s start with the big picture income—roughly what’s the household gross income per year right now?
Client 1: Household gross is 87500 a year.

Advisor: Okay. Let me just sanity-check the categories we’ve hit: income streams, assets including the house, debts including mortgage and non-mortgage, and protection. Anything you can think of that we missed that would create a payment or a balance?
Client 1: No, that’s basically it—no debts, just the accounts we listed and the house.
Advisor: Great. Next step after this is we’ll talk through goals and how aggressive you want to be with that investable bucket, but this baseline is clean.
Advisor: Okay—thanks. Since we’re about to talk goals and investing style, I want to anchor a couple of planning assumptions. First, just confirming: you’re based in the US, right?
Client 1: Yes, we’re in the US.

Client 2: I’m more cautious, honestly, but as a household we’ve been aggressive.
Advisor: That makes sense—two different comfort levels, one plan. Next one is taxes: do you know roughly what bracket you tend to land in most years?
Client 2: I think it’s 35%—like, the 35% bracket.
Advisor: Okay, 35%. And just so I’m not mixing terms—when you say 35%, you mean your tax bracket band, not like an investment fee or anything.
Client 2: Yes—tax bracket band is 35%.

Advisor: Great. We’ll unpack what’s inside that in a minute. Before we do, do either of you have any loans outstanding at all right now—mortgage, HELOC, anything like that?
Client 2: No, none. No loans outstanding.
Advisor: Okay. Let’s break down the income a bit so we understand the variability. William, what are the different income streams you’re seeing—like business income, bonuses, rental, that kind of thing?
Client 1: Yeah—so I’ve got monthly bonus income of 31850 annually, and monthly business income of 14450 annually.
Advisor: Okay—bonus and business income monthly. Anything else on your side?

Advisor: Great—thank you. And just to close the loop on what we covered earlier: we’ve got your income streams, the accounts including the house, no loans outstanding at all, and no protection policies in place right now. Next we’ll map goals to that aggressive approach with some guardrails.
Advisor: Alright. Before we get into goals, I want to make sure the household basics are right—just the stuff that drives planning assumptions.
Advisor: William, Margare—are you two legally married, or partnered another way?
Client 1: We’re married.
Advisor: Got it—thank you. And where are you based day-to-day? Which state are you filing from most of the year?

Client 1: We’re married.
Advisor: Got it—thank you. And where are you based day-to-day? Which state are you filing from most of the year?
Client 2: NJ.
Advisor: New Jersey—okay. And in the household, is it just the two of you as adults, or are there any other adults living with you?
Client 1: It’s just us—2 adults.
